The graph indicates no upstream services for the symptom, but the presence of multiple outbound calls means a root cause in any dependency is a structurally valid propagation source. result.synthesis_reasoning: All sources converge on the idea that the root cause is unlikely to be frontend itself (log agent found no errors, TCB-RCA centers on frontend only due to no observed timing anomalies). Topology confirms frontend has multiple downstream dependencies, making any of them plausible unobserved root causes in the absence of frontend logs. The log evidence is the strongest constraint against frontend-root causes, while topology provides a structured set of plausible downstream root causes. Given the absence of direct logs from downstream services in the observed window, we assign higher priority to unobserved downstream candidates that are structurally plausible (cartservice first, then productcatalogservice, then shippingservice). This synthesis treats the lack of logs as a significant limitation, while still respecting topology constraints and the unobserved-root-cause principle.
